title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement Douglas Morrison. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired [Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder][https://github.com/mozilla/inclusion]. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Getting Started with rSuStaIn
text: rSuStaIn package provides R interface pySuStaIn Python package Subtype Stage Inference (SuStaIn). SuStaIn unsupervised learning algorithm identifies disease subtypes stages cross-sectional data.

title: Getting Started with rSuStaIn
text: First, install R package GitHub: , install pySuStaIn Python package: create Python virtual environment called “r-pySuStaIn” install pySuStaIn dependencies.

title: Getting Started with rSuStaIn
text: SuStaIn requires: Patient data: data frame biomarker measurements subject Biomarker levels: list defining possible levels biomarker Probability correct classification: Estimates measurement accuracy
code: # Example data structure patient_data <- data.frame(   ID = paste0("Patient_", 1:100),   biomarker1 = sample(c("Normal", "Mild", "Severe"), 100, replace = TRUE),   biomarker2 = sample(c("Normal", "Mild", "Severe"), 100, replace = TRUE),   biomarker3 = sample(c("Normal", "Mild", "Severe"), 100, replace = TRUE) )  # Define biomarker levels biomarker_levels <- list(   biomarker1 = c("Normal", "Mild", "Severe"),   biomarker2 = c("Normal", "Mild", "Severe"),   biomarker3 = c("Normal", "Mild", "Severe") )

title: Getting Started with rSuStaIn
code: # Run SuStaIn results <- run_OSA(   patient_data = patient_data,   biomarker_levels = biomarker_levels,   SuStaInLabels = names(biomarker_levels),   N_startpoints = 25,        # Number of startpoints for optimization   N_S_max = 3,               # Maximum number of subtypes   N_iterations_MCMC = 1e5,   # MCMC iterations   output_folder = "output",   dataset_name = "my_analysis" )

title: Getting Started with rSuStaIn
text: results object contains: samples_sequence: Sampled biomarker event sequences subtype samples_f: Sampled subtype frequencies ml_subtype: Maximum likelihood subtype assignment subject prob_ml_subtype: Probability maximum likelihood subtype ml_stage: Maximum likelihood stage assignment subject prob_ml_stage: Probability maximum likelihood stage
code: # View subtype assignments head(results$ml_subtype)  # View stage assignments head(results$ml_stage)

title: Getting Started with rSuStaIn
text: ’ve already run SuStaIn Python pickle files, can extract results:
code: results <- extract_results_from_pickle(   n_s = 3,                    # Number of subtypes   dataset_name = "my_data",   output_folder = "output" )

title: Getting Started with rSuStaIn
text: can perform cross-validation assess model fit:
code: results <- run_OSA(   patient_data = patient_data,   biomarker_levels = biomarker_levels,   SuStaInLabels = names(biomarker_levels),   N_startpoints = 25,   N_S_max = 3,   N_CV_folds = 10,           # Enable 10-fold cross-validation   output_folder = "output",   dataset_name = "my_analysis" )  # Access CV results cv_results <- attr(results, "CV")

title: Getting Started with rSuStaIn
text: can specify custom probabilities correct classification:
code: prob_correct <- compute_prob_correct(   biomarker_levels = biomarker_levels,   # Customize parameters here )  results <- run_OSA(   patient_data = patient_data,   prob_correct = prob_correct,   biomarker_levels = biomarker_levels,   # ... other parameters )

title: Getting Started with rSuStaIn
text: Young, . L., Vogel, J. W., Aksman, L. M., Wijeratne, P. ., Eshaghi, ., Oxtoby, N. P., … & Alzheimer’s Disease Neuroimaging Initiative. (2021). Ordinal SuStaIn: Subtype Stage Inference Clinical Scores, Visual Ratings, Ordinal Data. Frontiers Artificial Intelligence, 4, 613261.

what: R Wrapper for Python pySuStaIn Package
title: R Wrapper for Python pySuStaIn Package
text: rSuStaIn package provides R wrapper pySuStaIn Python package, implements Subtype Stage Inference (SuStaIn) algorithm. SuStaIn unsupervised learning algorithm identifies disease subtypes stages cross-sectional data.

title: R Wrapper for Python pySuStaIn Package
text: package provides R wrappers three main SuStaIn algorithms: Ordinal SuStaIn (run_OSA): ordinal/scored biomarker data Z-score SuStaIn (run_ZscoreSustain): continuous biomarker measurements Mixture Model SuStaIn (run_MixtureSustain): event-based disease progression modeling

title: R Wrapper for Python pySuStaIn Package
text: Install R package GitHub: install pySuStaIn Python package:

title: R Wrapper for Python pySuStaIn Package
code: library(rSuStaIn)  # For ordinal/scored biomarker data results <- run_OSA(   patient_data = your_data,   biomarker_levels = biomarker_levels,   SuStaInLabels = names(biomarker_levels),   N_startpoints = 25,   N_S_max = 3 )

title: R Wrapper for Python pySuStaIn Package
code: # For continuous biomarker measurements results <- run_ZscoreSustain(   data = your_biomarker_matrix,   biomarker_labels = biomarker_names,   N_startpoints = 25,   N_S_max = 3 )

title: R Wrapper for Python pySuStaIn Package
code: # For event-based disease progression modeling results <- run_MixtureSustain(   data = your_biomarker_matrix,   biomarker_labels = biomarker_names,   N_startpoints = 25,   N_S_max = 3 )

title: R Wrapper for Python pySuStaIn Package
text: See Getting Started vignette detailed usage examples.

title: R Wrapper for Python pySuStaIn Package
text: Young, . L., Vogel, J. W., Aksman, L. M., Wijeratne, P. ., Eshaghi, ., Oxtoby, N. P., … & Alzheimer’s Disease Neuroimaging Initiative. (2021). Ordinal SuStaIn: Subtype Stage Inference Clinical Scores, Visual Ratings, Ordinal Data. Frontiers Artificial Intelligence, 4, 613261.

title: Install the pySuStaIn python package from github — install_pySuStaIn
text: envname name, full path, environment Python packages installed. NULL (default), active environment set RETICULATE_PYTHON_ENV variable used; unset, r-reticulate environment used. python_version requested Python version. Ignored attempting install Python virtual environment. method Installation method. default, "auto" automatically finds method work local environment. Change default force specific installation method. Note "virtualenv" method available Windows. pip Boolean; use pip package installation? relevant Conda environments used, otherwise packages installed Conda repositories. packages vector Python packages install. ... Arguments passed reticulate::py_install conda path conda executable. Use "auto" allow reticulate automatically find appropriate conda binary. See Finding Conda conda_binary() details. pip_ignore_installed,ignore_installed Boolean; whether pip ignore previously installed versions requested packages. Setting TRUE causes pip install latest versions dependencies requested environment. ensure dependencies satisfied package exists either site library previously installed different–potentially incompatible–distribution channel. (ignore_installed alias pip_ignore_installed, pip_ignore_installed takes precedence).

what: rSuStaIn: R Wrapper for Python 'pySuStaIn' Package — rSuStaIn-package
title: rSuStaIn: R Wrapper for Python 'pySuStaIn' Package — rSuStaIn-package
text: Provides R interface 'pySuStaIn' Python package (https://github.com/ucl-pond/pySuStaIn) Subtype Stage Inference (SuStaIn). SuStaIn unsupervised learning algorithm identifies disease subtypes stages cross-sectional data. package includes wrapper functions main SuStaIn algorithms: Ordinal SuStaIn ordinal/scored data, Z-score SuStaIn continuous biomarkers, Mixture Model SuStaIn event-based modeling.

title: rSuStaIn: R Wrapper for Python 'pySuStaIn' Package — rSuStaIn-package
text: Maintainer: Douglas Ezra Morrison demorrison@ucdavis.edu Authors: Douglas Ezra Morrison demorrison@ucdavis.edu Matthew Ponzini mdponzini@ucdavis.edu

what: Run Mixture Model SuStaIn Algorithm — run_MixtureSustain
title: Run Mixture Model SuStaIn Algorithm — run_MixtureSustain
text: Wrapper pySuStaIn's MixtureSustain class, implements event-based modeling using Gaussian Mixture Models.

title: Run Mixture Model SuStaIn Algorithm — run_MixtureSustain
code: run_MixtureSustain(   data,   biomarker_labels,   N_startpoints = 25,   N_S_max = 3,   N_iterations_MCMC = 100000L,   output_folder = "output",   dataset_name = "mixture_data",   use_parallel_startpoints = FALSE,   seed = 1L,   ... )

title: Run Mixture Model SuStaIn Algorithm — run_MixtureSustain
text: data matrix data.frame biomarker measurements rows = subjects, columns = biomarkers biomarker_labels character vector biomarker names N_startpoints integer number startpoints optimization (typically 25) N_S_max integer maximum number subtypes N_iterations_MCMC integer number MCMC iterations (typically 1e5 1e6) output_folder character save results dataset_name character name output files use_parallel_startpoints logical whether parallelize optimization seed integer random seed reproducibility ... additional arguments passed pySuStaIn$MixtureSustain()

title: Run Mixture Model SuStaIn Algorithm — run_MixtureSustain
text: list containing SuStaIn results

title: Run Mixture Model SuStaIn Algorithm — run_MixtureSustain
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Simulated mixture data set.seed(123) n_subjects <- 100 n_biomarkers <- 5 data <- matrix(rnorm(n_subjects * n_biomarkers, mean = 50, sd = 10),                 nrow = n_subjects, ncol = n_biomarkers) biomarker_labels <- paste0("Biomarker_", 1:n_biomarkers)  results <- run_MixtureSustain(   data = data,   biomarker_labels = biomarker_labels,   N_startpoints = 10,   N_S_max = 3,   N_iterations_MCMC = 1000,   output_folder = tempdir(),   dataset_name = "mixture_example" ) } # }

title: Run the Ordinal SuStaIn Algorithm (OSA) — run_OSA
code: run_OSA(   patient_data,   prob_correct,   prob_score = compute_prob_scores(dataset = patient_data, prob_correct = prob_correct,     biomarker_levels = biomarker_levels),   score_vals = build_score_vals(biomarker_levels),   biomarker_levels = get_levels(patient_data, varnames = SuStaInLabels),   SuStaInLabels = names(biomarker_levels),   N_startpoints = 25,   N_S_max = 8,   N_iterations_MCMC = 100000L,   output_folder = "output",   dataset_name = "sample_data",   use_parallel_startpoints = FALSE,   seed = 1L,   plot = FALSE,   N_CV_folds = 0,   CV_fold_nums = 1:N_CV_folds,   verbose = TRUE,   keep_data = TRUE,   fig_size = c(10, 10),   ... )

title: Run the Ordinal SuStaIn Algorithm (OSA) — run_OSA
text: patient_data patient biomarker data prob_correct probability correctly classifying underlying biomarker level: p(obs = true) prob_score array probability score subjects across biomarkers dim = number subjects x number biomarkers x number scores score_vals matrix specifying scores biomarker dim: number biomarkers x number scores biomarker_levels list containing levels biomarker SuStaInLabels names biomarkers list strings N_startpoints number startpoints use maximum likelihood step SuStaIn, typically 25 N_S_max maximum number subtypes, 1 N_iterations_MCMC number MCMC iterations, typically 1e5 1e6 can lower debugging output_folder save pickle files, etc. dataset_name naming pickle files use_parallel_startpoints boolean whether parallelize maximum likelihood loop seed random number seed python code plot logical() indicating whether construct PVD plots via python subroutines N_CV_folds number cross-validation folds create CV_fold_nums CV folds run (parallel processing) verbose logical() indicating whether print debugging information keep_data logical() indicating whether include ata return object fig_size python figure size, inches (width, height) ... additional arguments passed Python function pySuStaIn$OrdinalSustain()

text: Wrapper pySuStaIn's ZscoreSustain class, implements SuStaIn continuous biomarker data using z-score models.

title: Run Z-score SuStaIn Algorithm — run_ZscoreSustain
code: run_ZscoreSustain(   data,   biomarker_labels,   N_startpoints = 25,   N_S_max = 3,   N_iterations_MCMC = 100000L,   output_folder = "output",   dataset_name = "zscore_data",   use_parallel_startpoints = FALSE,   seed = 1L,   ... )

title: Run Z-score SuStaIn Algorithm — run_ZscoreSustain
text: data matrix data.frame biomarker measurements rows = subjects, columns = biomarkers biomarker_labels character vector biomarker names N_startpoints integer number startpoints optimization (typically 25) N_S_max integer maximum number subtypes N_iterations_MCMC integer number MCMC iterations (typically 1e5 1e6) output_folder character save results dataset_name character name output files use_parallel_startpoints logical whether parallelize optimization seed integer random seed reproducibility ... additional arguments passed pySuStaIn$ZscoreSustain()

title: Run Z-score SuStaIn Algorithm — run_ZscoreSustain
text: list containing SuStaIn results

title: Run Z-score SuStaIn Algorithm — run_ZscoreSustain
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Simulated data set.seed(123) n_subjects <- 100 n_biomarkers <- 5 data <- matrix(rnorm(n_subjects * n_biomarkers),                 nrow = n_subjects, ncol = n_biomarkers) biomarker_labels <- paste0("Biomarker_", 1:n_biomarkers)  results <- run_ZscoreSustain(   data = data,   biomarker_labels = biomarker_labels,   N_startpoints = 10,   N_S_max = 3,   N_iterations_MCMC = 1000,   output_folder = tempdir(),   dataset_name = "zscore_example" ) } # }

title: Run the Ordinal SuStaIn algorithm (OSA) or load results from presaved .rds file if available — run_and_save_OSA
text: dataset_name naming pickle files output_folder save pickle files, etc. verbose logical() indicating whether print debugging information N_S_max maximum number subtypes, 1 rerun whether force rerun python code rda_filename name rda file containing environment used run analyses ... Arguments passed run_OSA prob_score array probability score subjects across biomarkers dim = number subjects x number biomarkers x number scores score_vals matrix specifying scores biomarker dim: number biomarkers x number scores SuStaInLabels names biomarkers list strings N_startpoints number startpoints use maximum likelihood step SuStaIn, typically 25 N_iterations_MCMC number MCMC iterations, typically 1e5 1e6 can lower debugging use_parallel_startpoints boolean whether parallelize maximum likelihood loop seed random number seed python code plot logical() indicating whether construct PVD plots via python subroutines N_CV_folds number cross-validation folds create patient_data patient biomarker data CV_fold_nums CV folds run (parallel processing) keep_data logical() indicating whether include ata return object fig_size python figure size, inches (width, height) prob_correct probability correctly classifying underlying biomarker level: p(obs = true) biomarker_levels list containing levels biomarker

what: Major Changes - Refocused as General-Purpose Wrapper
title: rSuStaIn 0.1.0
text: Removed 193 FXTAS-specific R files (data cleaning, categorization, analysis functions) Removed FXTAS-specific examples, tests, data processing scripts Removed data-raw/, dev/, FXTAS analysis directories Package now contains 45 core SuStaIn wrapper utility files

title: rSuStaIn 0.1.0
text: Added run_ZscoreSustain() wrapper continuous biomarker data Added run_MixtureSustain() wrapper event-based disease progression modeling Updated package description reflect focus general SuStaIn algorithms Streamlined documentation focusing three main algorithm types

title: rSuStaIn 0.1.0
text: Renamed package fxtas rSuStaIn Updated DESCRIPTION new name, description focusing general SuStaIn wrapper functionality Updated URLs point ucdavis/rSuStaIn repository Renamed fxtas-package.R rSuStaIn-package.R Updated README focus general pySuStaIn wrapper usage Added “Getting Started” vignette examples Updated install_pySuStaIn() use official ucl-pond/pySuStaIn repository Generalized compute_prob_scores() id_var parameter Fixed Codecov slug reference workflows

title: rSuStaIn 0.1.0
text: package now exports focused set pySuStaIn wrappers: - install_pySuStaIn() - Install pySuStaIn Python package - run_OSA() - Run Ordinal SuStaIn Algorithm - run_ZscoreSustain() - Run Z-score SuStaIn - run_MixtureSustain() - Run Mixture Model SuStaIn - extract_results_from_pickle() - Extract results pickle files - Plus utilities probability computation, result formatting, visualization
